name: Campo de Calatrava and Valle de Alcudia Loop
name_original: Caracuel de Calatrava por el Valle de Alcudia — circular desde Corral de Calatrava
description: This difficult 84.7-mile road cycling route from Corral de Calatrava takes you through the scenic Valle de Alcudia, gaining 3642 feet.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Corral de Calatrava traverse a landscape characterized by the rolling plains of Campo de Calatrava and the varied terrain of Valle de Alcudia. The region features a mix of agricultural areas, historical sites like Alarcos Castle, and natural wetlands such as Laguna Peñarroya. Road cyclists can expect routes with significant elevation gains, indicating a hilly profile rather than flat expanses. Best road cycling routes around Corral De Calatrava The most popular road cycling route is Campo de Calatrava and Valle de Alcudia Loop, an 84.7 miles (136.3 km) trail that takes 5 hours 47 minutes to complete. This route offers extensive views across the Campo de Calatrava and through the Valle de Alcudia. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Abenójar, Saceruela, and Luciana Loop, a difficult 72.1 miles (116.0 km) path. This route navigates through rural towns and offers challenging climbs with over 1000 meters of elevation gain. Local road cyclists also love the By the Pozuelos de Calatrava and the Tabla de la Yedra — circular by Piedrabuena, a 65.0 miles (104.7 km) trail leading through the areas of Pozuelos de Calatrava and Tabla de la Yedra, often completed in about 4 hours 34 minutes. Road cycling around Corral de Calatrava is defined by rolling countryside, agricultural plains, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Corral de Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Corral de Calatrava?
A: Road cycling routes in Corral de Calatrava traverse a varied landscape, characterized by the rolling plains of Campo de Calatrava and the diverse terrain of Valle de Alcudia. You'll encounter a mix of agricultural areas and natural wetlands, with routes often featuring significant elevation gains, indicating a hilly profile rather than flat expanses.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Corral de Calatrava?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251008972/alarcos-castle-and-laguna-penarroya-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcos Castle and Laguna Peñarroya Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 31.6 km route with moderate elevation, perfect for those new to road cycling or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Experienced cyclists looking for a challenge will find several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251860322/campo-de-calatrava-and-valle-de-alcudia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de Calatrava and Valle de Alcudia Loop&lt;/a&gt; covers 136.3 km with over 1100 meters of elevation gain, offering extensive views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251040932/abenojar-saceruela-and-luciana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abenójar, Saceruela, and Luciana Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 116 km path with over 1000 meters of climbing through rural towns.

Q: Are most road cycling routes in Corral de Calatrava circular?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251860322/campo-de-calatrava-and-valle-de-alcudia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de Calatrava and Valle de Alcudia Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251040932/abenojar-saceruela-and-luciana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abenójar, Saceruela, and Luciana Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251026703/by-the-pozuelos-de-calatrava-and-the-tabla-de-la-yedra-circular-by-piedrabuena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;By the Pozuelos de Calatrava and the Tabla de la Yedra — circular by Piedrabuena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites or natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The region offers a blend of history and nature. You can cycle past historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3882572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcos Archaeological Park&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251008972/alarcos-castle-and-laguna-penarroya-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcos Castle and Laguna Peñarroya Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3533501" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old dam of the Guadiana River&lt;/a&gt; and various viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Posadilla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Corral de Calatrava?
A: Yes, the routes often provide scenic vistas. You can find viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2659589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Isidro Hermitage and Maares Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. The rolling plains and varied terrain naturally create many opportunities for picturesque stops.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Corral de Calatrava?
A: The best time for road cycling in Corral de Calatrava is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these months, the temperatures are milder and more pleasant for longer rides. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable if cycling during that season.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes are not listed, many routes pass through or near rural towns such as Abenójar, Saceruela, Luciana, and Piedrabuena. These towns typically offer local amenities where you can find refreshments, water, and a place to rest.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Corral de Calatrava area?
A: There are 13 road cycling routes available around Corral de Calatrava, catering to various ability levels from easy to difficult. This network offers a diverse range of experiences for cyclists.

Q: What makes road cycling in Corral de Calatrava unique?
A: Road cycling in Corral de Calatrava is defined by its blend of rolling countryside, agricultural plains, and historical landmarks. The region's routes offer a chance to explore the Campo de Calatrava and Valle de Alcudia, providing a unique mix of natural beauty and cultural heritage, often with challenging climbs and rewarding views.

Q: What is the average distance and elevation gain for road cycling routes here?
A: The routes vary significantly in length and elevation. You can find shorter routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251008972/alarcos-castle-and-laguna-penarroya-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcos Castle and Laguna Peñarroya Loop&lt;/a&gt; at 31.6 km with 234 meters of ascent, up to longer, more challenging rides such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251860322/campo-de-calatrava-and-valle-de-alcudia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de Calatrava and Valle de Alcudia Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 136.3 km long with over 1100 meters of elevation gain.
tips:
An initiative of the Junta de Castilla - La Mancha and the Laborvalía association has set itself the goal of decorating ten old grain silos with murals.

In this case, it is a work by the artist Okuda San Miguel, who, inspired by the province of La Mancha and the "Almodóvar girls", has incorporated some characters from the films of the La Mancha film director, being able to see Rossy de Palma or the poster of the movie "Bad Education".

Small municipality located on the road that connects Ciudad Real with Puertollano. On the so-called Mount Nogales are the remains of the castle, with a pentagonal keep and remains of a "U"-shaped wall. It is accessed by a path next to the hermitage of Santo Cristo, of popular typology.
Section of the CM-4115 road through the Dehesa Labrada, good track and beautiful scenery.
Immense valley that stretches over 100 kilometers in length and that, in addition, is on the border between the two provinces where the Río Valdeazogues crosses.

In the Alcudia Valley, we find the municipality of Almodóvar del Campo built on the slope of a volcano, so present in Campo de Calatrava, and whose crater is now a lagoon. The Arab footprint is the most emblematic of the city, leaving behind the memory of the Castle and above all the unique Parish of Our Lady of the Assumption. There you can see the first videomapping in Spain permanently installed in a closed space, under the theme of the life and work of San Juan de Ávila.

Monuments and centers of cultural interest are scattered throughout its streets: the Palermo Museum, the Casa-Convento de La Merced, the current home of the Hieronymite nuns, modernist in style and dating from the 19th century. Continuing the visit you can visit the Cervantina Library, inside which an infinite number of editions of Don Quixote are collected, in different languages, formats and periods, as well as canvases and drawings related to the Cervantine character. From here you can also see the Municipal Theatre, built in 1845 on the site of the old Carmelite Convent.
From the 14th or early 15th century. Built in three parts. The first one is made up of the presbytery, in the Romanesque style, while the central nave is in the Gothic style with a ribbed vault.

faqs.question: What level of fitness is required for this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a "hard road ride" and is ideal for experienced road cyclists. It requires a very good level of fitness due to its substantial length of approximately 136 kilometers and a significant elevation gain of 1,110 meters.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: While many roads in the Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona region are generally well-paved, this demanding route may include varied surfaces. You can expect a mix of numbered regional roads, but also potentially unpaved sections like "caminos" (paths or tracks), "vías de servicio" (service roads), "cañadas" (livestock trails), and "cordeles" (paths). Be prepared for some challenging segments.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this 136-kilometer route is around 5 hours and 47 minutes of active cycling time. However, this can vary significantly based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or rest.

faqs.question: Where does the route start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Corral de Calatrava, making it a circular tour. While specific parking details for Corral de Calatrava are not provided, as a starting point for a long road cycling route, you would typically find public parking options within the town.

faqs.question: What historical sites or natural features can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route takes you through the picturesque Valle de Alcudia, offering a blend of natural beauty and historical significance. You'll pass through settlements like Caracuel de Calatrava and the natural area of Valle de Alcudia. Other highlights include the Parish Church of Our Lady of the Assumption, Almodóvar del Campo and the Church of Our Lady of the Visitation.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Valle de Alcudia region can experience hot summers. For a challenging route of this length, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the extreme heat of summer and potential chill of winter.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a long and demanding road cycling route. While dogs are generally allowed in natural areas, bringing a dog on a 136-kilometer racebike tour is not practical or recommended. This route is best enjoyed by cyclists without canine companions.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for cycling in the Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the Valle de Alcudia, which is part of the Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona Natural Park. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads and established trails within the natural park. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several settlements, including Caracuel de Calatrava and Almodóvar del Campo. These towns would be your best bet for finding cafes, restaurants, or shops to refuel and rehydrate. It's always wise to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer, more remote sections.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of historical livestock trails. You'll encounter small portions of the Cañada Real Segoviana and the Cañada Real Soriana Oriental, which are traditional Spanish drovers' roads.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and difficulty, you should bring plenty of water, energy gels or bars, a repair kit (including spare tubes, pump, and tools), a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing for varying weather conditions. A GPS device with the route loaded is highly recommended, as some sections might be less clearly marked.
